notes: No formal opposition group identified. Housing crisis is the main community concern (rents surging with 6,000 construction workers arriving in a city of 100,000). TIME reported residents saying 'nobody can find a place to live.' Individual residents raised concerns at P&Z meetings but no organized petition or Facebook group was found specifically opposing the Abilene Stargate project.

No named opposition group found for the Middleton Township Meta project specifically. However, approximately 150 residents packed Wood County Planning Commission meetings demanding a moratorium on new data centers. Residents along Devils Hole Road organized informally. The broader Wood County data center opposition (spanning both Middleton Township and Bowling Green area entries) lacks a formal named organization. Fourteen people submitted formal opposition to the fast-tracked 350MW Apollo gas plant approval.

No formal opposition group, petition, or Facebook group found. Residents wearing 'No Eminent Domain' t-shirts packed city meetings, and an annexation battle emerged, but community sentiment was mixed -- many residents said the $7B deal was 'worth the growing pains.' Resident Renee Hutchinson expressed concerns about grid stability but did not begrudge the company.

The Change.org petition 'Stop Data Center Construction in Lackawanna County' (started by Scott Price, July 2025) has 2,015 signatures and covers Blakely and surrounding Lackawanna County communities. A separate local petition gathered roughly 800 signatures in Blakely itself. Residents organized via a community Facebook page and created/sold 'No Data Center' signs. About 100 people packed the Blakely Borough Building with 60 more outside and 210+ on Zoom. The project was withdrawn in September 2025. Better Path Coalition (co-founded by Karen Feridun) also supported PA-wide data center opposition.

A petition with 5,047 signatures was delivered to the College Station City Council. More than 75 people registered to speak in person. A TexAgs forum thread had over 20,000 views. Facebook posts drew unprecedented engagement. The City Council voted unanimously to reject the Priority Power land sale on September 11, 2025. No specific named opposition group was identified in reporting -- the opposition was grassroots and community-driven.

No formal opposition group, petition, or Facebook group found. Opposition is led primarily by Platte County Presiding Commissioner Scott Fricker, who publicly stated he 'can't support Project Kestrel' due to massive tax subsidies for wealthy corporations at the expense of Northland residents. KC Mayor Quinton Lucas's Facebook post about data centers drew 100+ comments. The project involves Port KC and six hyperscale data centers on 380 acres.

The Change.org petition 'STOP AI DATA CENTER Lufkin, TX' (started by Kaitlyn Armstrong, February 7, 2026) has 2,616 signatures with a goal of 5,000. The Lufkin city council denied an Oncor substation permit after resident opposition. Water usage claims were contested -- Amp Z says 500 gallons/day, opponents cited 20 million gallons/day (the former paper mill's capacity). Angelina County rejected a moratorium on data center development.

Two Change.org petitions exist: 'Stop Data Centers in Cumberland County -- Protect Fayetteville's Health and Water' (by Rashad Rahman, Dec 30 2025) has 1,449 signatures (goal: 1,500), and a second titled 'Stop the Data Center in Cumberland County.' ESS markets the project as 'NetZero' despite relying on fossil fuel combustion. The Fayetteville Planning Commission denied the ESS data center conceptual site plan. About 50 people attended a public forum hosted by the Fayetteville Cumberland County Economic Development Corporation. ESS previously had a similar proposal rejected in Tarboro and sued to overturn that decision.

No named opposition group, formal petition, or Facebook group found. However, community opposition was overwhelming: emails to the Chandler City Council ran 20-to-1 against the project, with over 250 emails in opposition and only 10 in support. One couple sent out 2,000 postcards to organize opposition. Former Senator Kyrsten Sinema spoke in favor of the project as a paid representative of the AI Infrastructure Coalition. The City Council voted unanimously (7-0) to reject Active Infrastructure's proposal on December 11, 2025.

No named opposition group found. Approximately 150 residents packed a Wood County Planning Commission meeting in January 2026. Residents requested a moratorium on data centers, but the Wood County Economic Development Commission said the county lacks legal authority under Ohio law to impose one. The opposition is the same broader movement as the Middleton Township entry -- both relate to Meta's data center in Wood County. The 350MW 'Apollo' gas plant was fast-tracked by the Ohio Power Siting Board with no public hearings.

opposition_groups: Citizens for Open Governance, Columbia County Georgia
notes: Citizens for Open Governance, Columbia County Georgia organized community pushback against the White Oak Technology Park. The Columbia County Observer (by Karin Parham) covers meetings and opposition on Substack. Over 200 residents turned out to oppose data centers, with 38 different speakers against. Gregory P. Guido Jr. filed two judicial review petitions (Feb 25, 2026) challenging the Board of Commissioners' approval, alleging procedural defects, incomplete Development of Regional Impact review, and conflicts with the comprehensive plan. A petition opposing the related Pumpkin Technology Campus gathered 195 signatures. The Board approved White Oak and Pumpkin Center but rejected the Byrd Farm data center.

No named opposition group or formal petition found. Residents have been 'banding together on social media, bringing awareness' according to resident Candy May. Dozens of residents packed a planning and zoning meeting, with overflow rooms needed. The public hearing was rescheduled to March 23, 2026 due to a paperwork error (duplicated parcel numbers). Amazon plans to construct a $2B data center on 330+ acres off Vans Valley Road in the Sunbury Industrial Park. A lawsuit aims to block Amazon's planned array of 228 fuel cells at a nearby data center.

No named opposition group or formal petition found specific to the ABEI Energy proposal in Franklin County, Indiana. The Franklin County Area Planning Commission enacted a two-month moratorium on new data center building permits. ABEI presented plans for 300 acres with up to 15 structures (6-7 stories each), potentially reaching 860 MW over 15 years. All 18 public comments received were opposed. Residents questioned ABEI's employment projections of 4,000-6,000 jobs, noting large facilities can operate with far fewer workers. ABEI is also proposing data centers in Starke County, Indiana.

The Change.org petition 'Protect Pacific and the Meramec Valley from a Massive Data Center' has 309 signatures (goal: 500). Two additional Change.org petitions exist: 'Stop data center development at Crooked Creek Beef Farm - Pacific, MO' and 'Demand Transparency on Data Center Records in Pacific, MO Under Missouri Sunshine Law.' Hundreds packed Pacific City Hall and Pacific High School. Community groups hired an environmental attorney. The Missouri Department of Conservation flagged impacts on bald eagles, bats, and other species. Beltline Energy withdrew its rezoning request minutes into a special meeting on Feb 26, 2026, citing incomplete funding agreements. Notably, the land is owned by a Franklin County official.

No named opposition group or formal petition found. Resident Dorothy Landt of Palo called for a moratorium or ban, questioning why Linn County became 'a dumping ground for soon-to-be obsolete technology.' Rather than a moratorium, Linn County enacted one of the nation's most comprehensive data center zoning ordinances, requiring environmental reviews, noise limits, water usage plans, visual screening, and decommissioning bonds. Google pulled out of its plan to build in unincorporated Linn County on March 4, 2026, shifting to the city of Palo's jurisdiction instead.

No named opposition group or formal petition found. Dozens of residents filled Fassett Junior High School for a town hall, voicing concerns about traffic, land use, and utility costs. The city sold 170+ acres to Capacity LLC. A controversial council vote (3-2 with one abstention) on extending Capacity LLC's development agreement was initially thought to have failed but was later ruled by the city's law director to have passed. Facebook posts about the project became contentious, with accusations of improper behavior from both sides.

No opposition group, petition, or Facebook group found. Pacifico Energy's GW Ranch in Pecos County was approved by TCEQ as 'the largest permitted data center campus in the United States' at 7.65 GW on 8,000+ acres near Fort Stockton. It is authorized to release more than 12,000 tons/year of regulated air pollutants and up to 33 million tons/year of greenhouse gases (equal to nearly 5% of Canada's annual total). The remote, sparsely populated location in the Permian Basin oil patch may explain the lack of organized opposition.

The Change.org petition 'Stop data center in Muscogee County' has 1,814 signatures (goal: 2,500). Habitat Real Estate Partners' 'Project Ruby' would require 600 MW of power -- more than all of Columbus currently uses. The $5.18B project would span 987 acres in Upatoi. Citizens lined up at public agenda meetings to voice concerns about electricity demand, water resources, noise, traffic, stormwater runoff, and property values. The Development Authority is partnering with Habitat Partners.

No opposition group, petition, or Facebook group found specific to the 108 MW data center in Cobb County (by MMM Acquisitions LLC at 1751 Bells Ferry Road, approved by Marietta City Council). However, Cobb County commissioners voted unanimously in February 2026 to impose a 180-day moratorium on new data center permits in unincorporated areas, citing noise, air quality, aesthetics, and utility impacts. This moratorium is related to broader concerns and the separate Project Sail ($17B, Coweta County) which prompted a petition with 1,750 signatures and a Facebook group 'Stop Project Sail Newnan Data Center' with 2,500+ members -- but that is a different project in a neighboring county.
